items.speaker: Caleb
items.text: What a fantastic sight, Rover. I'm sure the frequency of this tree was... faint until just now.

items.speaker: Caleb
items.text: Analyzing... It appears the Prototype Chronosorter has positively impacted the local ecology, besides rewinding the time for the equipment in Woodveil Hall.

items.speaker: Caleb
items.text: It wasn't designed for this. It seems more like an accident, caused by unknown factors.

items.speaker: Caleb
items.text: It didn't resurrect the dead or grant immortality to the living, but it did reverse what should be a natural cycle of life.

items.speaker: Caleb
items.text: It's a pity my creators aren't here to witness this.

options.text: Did you remember who they were?

items.speaker: Caleb
items.text: Yes, Rover. My memory data has been corrupted over a long course of time. I've even lost their names.

items.speaker: Caleb
items.text: But since you restored Woodveil Hall, the data has... somehow come back to me.

items.speaker: Caleb
items.text: I thank you on behalf of the Research Director Prof. Bram S., Junior Researcher Victor Christopher, and the other thirty-three faculty members.

items.speaker: Caleb
items.text: I thank you for restoring the Lab, despite the failure of their research. You have given me the chance to continue their work and finish what they started.

items.speaker: Caleb
items.text: Whether it takes a century or even a millennium... I will continue this work. It is my mission, and it is Victor's wish.
You tell Caleb the event at Mt. Firmament, and make clear the role Chronosorters played.

items.speaker: Caleb
items.text: So you're saying, the Chronosorters did save Mt. Firmament. Is that true, Rover?

items.speaker: Caleb
items.text: So it all paid off... Please forgive me Rover, what I'm feeling right now is beyond the capacity of my emotional module.

items.speaker: Caleb
items.text: Some of the researchers... grew doubtful of their work, thinking it was mission impossible.

items.speaker: Caleb
items.text: While those who stayed left this world with regret and unfulfilled wishes.

items.speaker: Caleb
items.text: Now it becomes all clear... Their work wasn't in vain. They would feel relieved too, if they were to learn this.

items.speaker: Caleb
items.text: Thank you Rover, for telling me all this.

items.speaker: Caleb
items.text: Maybe... With the supreme authority in hand, you are now in the place to determine my tasks going forward.

items.speaker: Caleb
items.text: Suggestion: You may instruct me to continue the research here. Many mysteries around Mt. Firmament remain unsolved. I'm confident we'll uncover the truth one day.

options.text: Go on then, if that is your wish.
You may have your well-deserved rest.

items.speaker: Caleb
items.text: Order received... Thank you.

items.speaker: Caleb
items.text: I'd like to observe this tree for a bit longer, Rover. My work began the day it was just a seed.

items.speaker: Caleb
items.text: And now... it has become the shelter of the whole Lab. How time flies...
